title: Multimodal dataset from the CMx7-MM Experiment
description: This multimodal neuroimaging dataset comprises simultaneous EEG, functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), and physiological recordings (ECG and EMG) from 30 healthy adults performing a hierarchy of cognitive, motor, and combined cognitive-motor tasks. The dataset includes seven task conditions ranging from isolated mental arithmetic and motor imagery to integrated cognitive-motor challenges, along with behavioral measures (push-button responses, Biodex torque and kinematic data) and subjective ratings of sleepiness and cognitive load. Raw data from three sessions per participant are organized according to BIDS standards, providing a comprehensive resource for investigating neural mechanisms underlying cognitive-motor integration.
methods_description: Data were acquired from 30 right-handed healthy adults across three sessions (~30 minutes each). EEG was recorded using 32 channels in the 10-20 system; fNIRS covered prefrontal and sensorimotor regions. Physiological signals included ECG (Delsys Trigno) and EMG. Participants performed seven tasks in randomized order: mental arithmetic, 2-back auditory task, motor imagery, passive motor (Biodex-driven), active motor, combined N-back arithmetic, and full cognitive-motor task. Auditory stimuli (digits 0-9 and beeps) were delivered via MATLAB/Psychtoolbox. Subjective measures included Karolinska Sleepiness Scale ratings and cognitive load assessments. All recordings are raw exports without offline preprocessing.
